module-1-terminal-map-project.label: Project: Terminal Map
module-1-terminal-map-project.summary: Inspect a small field kit folder map and prove you can find each checkpoint.

sketch0.title: Your goal is a clear terminal map

sketch2.title: The checker looks at your workspace

ci_terminal_map_final.title: Confirm the south note from the map

try_module_1_terminal_map_project_sketch.title: Check a small route map
try_module_1_terminal_map_project_sketch.prompt: Run `ls demo-map` and look for `east`, `west`, and `notes.txt`.

try_module_1_terminal_map_project_sketch.title: Check the west hall
try_module_1_terminal_map_project_sketch.prompt: Use the demo map to reach `demo-map/west`, then run `ls`.

sc-project-command.title: Which command confirms your current folder?
sc-project-command.hint: It prints the full path of your current folder.

help.hint_2: Choose the command that shows where you are.

sc-check-work.title: What should you do before clicking Check Answer?
sc-check-work.hint: Use `pwd` and `ls` to check your location and nearby files.

help.concept: A quick `pwd` or `ls` check catches most navigation mistakes.
help.hint_2: Check the files nearby before submitting.

mc-project-tools.title: Choose tools used in this project.
mc-project-tools.prompt: This project uses the navigation and inspection commands from Module 1.
mc-project-tools.hint: Think about commands that show location, list files, and move folders.

help.concept: The field kit check uses `pwd`, `ls`, and `cd`.
help.hint_1: Pick location, listing, and navigation commands.
help.hint_2: Do not pick permission or installation commands.

sc-project-focus.title: What is the main focus of Course 1 projects?
sc-project-focus.prompt: You are learning terminal commands, not Bash scripts yet.
sc-project-focus.hint: You are learning terminal commands, not Bash scripts yet.

help.concept: You are learning terminal commands, not Bash scripts yet.
help.hint_1: You are learning terminal commands, not Bash scripts yet.
help.hint_2: Review the command names from the lesson, then choose the option that matches the task.

ci-terminal-map-base.title: List the field kit folders
ci-terminal-map-base.prompt: Run `ls field-kit` and confirm you see `north`, `south`, and `logs`.
ci-terminal-map-base.hint: List the folder named `field-kit` from the workspace root.

help.concept: `ls folder-name` shows what is inside that folder.

ci-terminal-map-markers.title: Check the north note
ci-terminal-map-markers.prompt: Use `cd` to enter `field-kit/north`, then run `pwd` and `ls`.
ci-terminal-map-markers.hint: Move into the north folder before listing its contents.

help.concept: Use `cd` to move, `pwd` to confirm, and `ls` to inspect.

ci-terminal-map-final.title: Confirm the south note from the map
ci-terminal-map-final.prompt: Use the field kit map to reach `field-kit/south`, then run `pwd` and `ls`.
ci-terminal-map-final.hint: Return to the workspace root if needed, then enter the south folder.

help.concept: A path like `field-kit/south` lets you jump into a nested folder.
help.hint_1: Run `cd field-kit/south` from the workspace root.

try-map-list-demo.title: Check a small route map
try-map-list-demo.prompt: Run `ls demo-map` and look for `east`, `west`, and `notes.txt`.

help.concept: Listing a prepared folder helps you plan your next move.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

help.concept: Use `pwd` after `cd` so you know exactly where you are.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

try-map-check-west.title: Check the west hall
try-map-check-west.prompt: Use the demo map to reach `demo-map/west`, then run `ls`.
try-map-check-west.hint: Move to `demo-map/west`, then list the folder.

help.concept: Moving and listing confirms both your path and the folder contents.
help.hint_1: Move to `demo-map/west`, then list the folder.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

project-goal.title: Plan the file map before typing
project-goal.bodyMarkdown:
A clear folder map makes terminal work calmer. Before creating folders, picture the final shape.

For this project, you are setting up a field kit with places for north-side notes, south-side notes, and logs.

project-workflow.title: Put each file where someone would expect it
project-workflow.bodyMarkdown: Folders are useful only when their names match the job. A person opening `north`, `south`, or `logs` should immediately know where to look.

project-checking.title: Check the handoff before you leave
project-checking.bodyMarkdown: After creating files, list the folders and make sure nothing is missing. A quick check with `ls` catches small path mistakes.
